programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: The first invasion of the Italian Campaign of WWII: Operation Husky.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: A secret British regiment in 1942 led a daring raid on a Nazi-occupied Libyan harbor.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: An elite squad of Norwegians destroy a power plant in 1943 that the Nazis may have been using for nuclear weapons research.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Indonesia AirAsia Flight 8501 stalls and crashes into the Java Sea while flying through a thunderstorm.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: A modern turboprop nosedives towards the North Sea after being struck by lightning, an unusual feature in the plane may explain why.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: SilkAir Flight 185 crashes into the Musi River in Indonesia after abruptly diving from its cruise altitude.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Three flights headed for disaster are saved at the last moment by heroes in the cockpit.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Pilots of Eastern Airline Flight 401 try to determine why both landing-gear lights do not function on an L-1011, tragically distracting them.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: An Alitalia airliner crashes into a forested hillside just a few miles from a Zurich runway.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: This 50-year-old ocean liner offers off-season Baltic tours, battling unpredictable weather to deliver one-of-a-kind experiences.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: One of Holland America Line's largest cruisers makes its debut in the Caribbean, highlighted by a visit to an exclusive private island resort.

programme.desc: Experience romance on a mega cruiser that pampers its guests with the best of Italian design and American convenience.

programme.desc: The world's latest, most-advanced expedition cruise ship Roald Amundsen whisks passengers on an adventurous voyage to Antarctica.

programme.desc: Japan's largest cruise ship brings East and West together on a cruise featuring cherry.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: We hop aboard a seven-day cruise to the Canary Islands and Madeira aboard the first large liner to run purely on cleaner-burning liquefied natural gas.

programme.desc: Hector Rail's trains haul timber to a pulp mill in Ostrand, Sweden on some of the most difficult railways in the country.

programme.desc: A trip through Lima details the voyage's breathtaking scenery and engineering challenges.

programme.title: Mighty Trains
programme.desc: The Glacier Express features cutting-edge technology to pull the train up the steepest slopes on its route across the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: Mighty Ships
programme.desc: The Norwegian Breakaway cruise ship sets sail on her first voyage, and her captain faces a tough decision when a North Atlantic tempest forms.

programme.title: Mighty Ships
programme.desc: Follow the building of the Norwegian Breakaway at Germany's Meyer-Werft shipyard; it is the largest ship they've ever built and they have only 18 months to finish it.

programme.title: Mighty Ships
programme.desc: The world's most adventurous cruise ship, MS Fram, battles ice-choked fjords of Greenland to visit wildlife and icebergs.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Sailing from Chile and Peru to Ecuador and Costa Rica, the luxurious Seven Seas Explorer cruise ship is full of marble, granite and almost 500 chandeliers.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: Norwegian Joy sails amid icebergs in Alaska's Inside Passage, treating guests to glaciers, humpback whales, and the largest zip line in the world.

programme.title: Mighty Planes
programme.desc: SOFIA, the largest airborne observatory in the world, boasts a 17-ton telescope capable of making observations impossible for ground-based telescopes.

programme.desc: On Europe's high-speed rail network, between Barcelona and Madrid, crews work to expand the infrastructure for a new train.

programme.desc: We follow passengers and crew aboard Viking Star during its two-week tour from Barcelona to its home port in Bergen, Norway.

programme.desc: The new Viking Sea cruise ship whisks guests away on a journey across the Arctic Circle and into the heart of Norway.

programme.desc: VIA Rail's passenger train travels between Vancouver and Toronto on four-day, four-night journey that cater to everyone.

programme.desc: Travel aboard the colourful trains of the State Railway of Thailand and explore the famed Death Railway; the trains travel through ancient areas, historic tunnels, and raging rivers.

programme.title: Mighty Cruise Ships
programme.desc: The Carnival Vista is the new flagship for one of the world's largest cruise lines, carrying more than 4,600 passengers and boasting 10 restaurants, nearly 2,000 staterooms and a crew of 1,400.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Traveling to New York, Denver and Nice, France to visit Little Island, the Denver Museum of Art and La Tête Carrée; the secrets to how they were built is revealed.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Visiting the Vancouver House, Norway's Under restaurant and the Seattle Central Library to discover how these visionary buildings were created.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: A towering bridge in France; upside-down hotel in China; Italy's forest into the sky.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: The engineering secrets behind the world's steepest mountain railway, a remodeled 145-year-old museum and a futuristic condo.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Building an eco-friendly visitor center in the permafrost on Colorado's Pikes Peak tests the team. A museum is constructed in a disused dry dock that's in danger of collapse.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Traveling from Canada to Belgium to the Southern U.S. to examine an eye-popping school, museum and railway station and reveal how they were built.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: Mike joins the Assault Craft Unit 5, the U.S. Navy's team for Landing Craft Air Cushions (LCACs); LCACs carry heavy vehicles and troops onto beaches; Mike helps fly & fix an overhauled LCAC during a U.S. Marines beach assault training mission.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: Nevada has over 40,000 abandoned mines posing dangers; Mike joins contractors for a week to secure five sites; alongside biologists, they explore and document wildlife; reaching these isolated mines requires barges, helicopters and bulldozers.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: A ride on the Panama Canal Railway reveals the track repairs, engine overhauls and dangerous searches involved in keeping vital freight runs rolling.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That?
programme.desc: A crew and its massive air crane helicopter haul millions of pounds of timber while battling the elements and handling emergency repairs.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Uncovering the design and engineering secrets behind the Grand Canyon Skywalk, the Audain Museum and more.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: The inside stories behind a new, horseshoe-shaped complex in Rotterdam, a rock-shaped opera house in China and a makeover of the British Museum.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Exploring the engineering and architectural masterpieces like The Copper in Manhattan to Gardens By the Bay in Singapore.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Engineers grapple with an apartment building, ripped apart by a man-made canyon; the Gateway Arch takes shape in St. Louis; an unpromising plot in Copenhagen, Denmark, is transformed by a gleaming new triangular office.

programme.title: How Did They Build That?
programme.desc: Alien port authority in Belgium; ultrathin skyscraper in Manhattan; hanging museum in Scotland.

programme.title: Disasters Engineered
programme.desc: Experts examine how engineering failures allowed Hurricane Katrina to devastate New Orleans and cause the collapse of the Tacoma Narrows Bridge.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: Human flaws cause three plane crashes in England, Colorado and Minnesota.

programme.title: Air Disasters
programme.desc: On 21 February 2008, Santa Bárbara Airlines Flight 518 strays off course and flies into the side of a mountain shortly after takeoff.
